answer: The Amazon River is 6,992 kilometers long. It is the longest river in the world. Its source is Mount Mismi in Peru.

results.title: How Long Is the Amazon River? | Britannica
results.snippet: It is the largest river by volume, and its basin is home to the Amazon Rainforest, the world’s richest and most-varied biological reservoir. Most scientists believe the South American river is at least 4,000 miles (6,400 km) long—still shorter than the Nile, which is widely held to be the world’s longest river at about 4,132 miles (6,650 km). In fact, even the definition of what constitutes a source has been debated—though most scientists agree it is the farthest point of continuously flowing water that can travel to the river’s mouth. Concerning its end point, the Amazon has three main outlets to the Atlantic Ocean: two on the northern side of Marajó Island in Brazil and one to the island’s south that joins the Pará River. They determined that Apacheta Creek—which is 6 miles (10 km) longer than the nearby Carhuasanta—is actually the Amazon’s source. In the end, the Amazon River’s total length was found to be 4,345 miles (6,992 km).

results.title: The #Amazon River is 6,992.06... - Engineering Facts
results.snippet: ## Engineering Facts's Post. ### **Engineering Facts**. The #Amazon River is 6,992.06 kilometers long, originating at the foot of Mount Mismi in Peru and flowing into the Atlantic Ocean in Brazil. For centuries, the Amazon and the Nile have competed for the title of the longest river in the world. A study published by Brazil’s National Institute for Space Research (INPE) in 2008 determined that the Amazon is the longest river on the planet. Backed by satellite imagery, the analysis by INPE’s Remote Sensing Department found that “the Amazon River is 6,992.06 kilometers long, while the Nile is 6,852.15 kilometers long.” The Amazon’s abundant tributaries flow through Colombia, Venezuela, Bolivia, Ecuador, Guyana, French Guiana and Suriname.

results.title: Amazon River - 7 Wonders
results.snippet: # Amazon River. The Amazon River is the largest river of the world and its basin is also the largest drainage basin of the planet; since, it covers an area of 6 475 000 square kilometers (2500 000 square miles) in Peru, Brazil, Ecuador Colombia, Venezuela and Bolivia. For a long time, the Amazon was considered the second longest river of the world after the Nile; but, recently, the discovery of a new tributary of the Amazon in Peru has caused that many scientists consider currently the Amazon the longest river of the World. According to a Brazilian scientist, using the Mismi mountain in the region of Arequipa at south of Peru as the origin of the Amazon, the river is the longest river of the world; since, it is around 105 kilometers (65 miles) longer than the Nile River. Currently the Amazon River basin is the main source of oxygen of the world and one of the most important patrimonies of the humanity.
